mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.8% in March 2011 suggests ongoing post-recession labor market recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero signals extended monetary accommodation that suppresses risk-free rates and incentivizes equity allocation, producing sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% in March 2011 reflects zero lower bound policy
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal accelerating consumer demand and inventory replenishment cycles, which boost corporate revenues and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389.3 billion in March 2011 shows consumer spending activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of instances when RSAFS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals rising capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which indicates strengthening business investment and future earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.59 in March 2011 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 52% of instances when INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market tightening that accelerates wage growth and consumer purchasing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.7 million and UNRATE at 8.8% in March 2011 show mixed labor signals
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase with simultaneous job creation and unemployment decline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts growth combined with rising retail sales signals broad-based demand acceleration across residential and consumer sectors, which drives equity valuations higher through earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 500k annualized AND RSAFS increases > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 479k and RSAFS at 389.3B in early 2011 show demand pressures
regime_hint: post-recession demand recovery with synchronized housing and consumption growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 62% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% signals monetary policy constraints that will force future rate increases, causing equity investors to discount future cash flows more heavily and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 223.49 in March 2011 reflects post-crisis inflation pressures
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 48% of instances when CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production decline combined with rising unemployment signals demand destruction and profit margin compression, which causes equity investors to reduce valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.59 and UNRATE at 8.8% in March 2011 show supply-side weakness risk
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession with simultaneous supply contraction and labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
